name: Apple
semantic_description: Crisp and sweet red apples. Excellent source of vitamin C and dietary fiber. Great as a healthy snack anytime of day or in fruit salads. Low calorie and naturally sweet.

name: Banana
semantic_description: High energy fruit packed with potassium and natural sugars. Perfect pre-workout snack or quick breakfast on the go. Easy to digest. Good for muscle recovery and heart health.

name: Mango
semantic_description: King of fruits. Rich in vitamin A and C. Ideal for summer desserts, smoothies, juices, and milkshakes. Sweet and aromatic Alphonso variety prized across India.

name: Orange
semantic_description: Juicy citrus fruit loaded with vitamin C and antioxidants. Great for boosting immunity. Ideal for fresh juice, fruit salads, or eating as a healthy snack.

name: Grapes
semantic_description: Sweet and crunchy seedless green grapes. Rich in antioxidants and vitamins. Perfect as a quick healthy snack or added to fruit salads and desserts.

name: Watermelon
semantic_description: Over 90% water content makes this ideal for summer hydration. Low calorie and naturally sweet. Great for juice, smoothies, or eating chilled on a hot day.

name: Papaya
semantic_description: Rich in digestive enzyme papain. Excellent source of vitamin A and C. Promotes digestion and gut health. Good for breakfast or as a light snack.

name: Pineapple
semantic_description: Tropical fruit rich in bromelain enzyme which aids digestion. High in vitamin C. Great for smoothies, desserts, and as a refreshing snack.

name: Pomegranate
semantic_description: One of the most antioxidant-rich fruits. Supports heart health and lowers inflammation. Pomegranate seeds can be added to salads, juices, or eaten directly.

name: Strawberry
semantic_description: Bright red berries rich in vitamin C and folate. Low calorie and naturally sweet. Perfect for breakfast with oats, in smoothies, or as a healthy dessert topping.

name: Kiwi
semantic_description: Nutrient-dense fruit with more vitamin C than an orange. High in fiber and folate. Supports digestion and immunity. Great as a breakfast fruit or snack.

name: Guava
semantic_description: High fiber tropical fruit with four times more vitamin C than oranges. Excellent for digestion, immunity, and weight management. Best eaten fresh.

name: Pear
semantic_description: Mild sweet fruit high in dietary fiber. Gentle on the stomach. Good for digestive health. Best eaten fresh as a snack or added to fruit bowls.

name: Litchi
semantic_description: Juicy summer fruit with sweet floral flavor. Good source of vitamin C and B vitamins. Best enjoyed fresh. Popular in desserts and juices.

name: Coconut
semantic_description: Versatile tropical fruit. Coconut water is excellent for hydration and electrolytes. Coconut flesh is used in cooking curries and desserts. High in healthy fats.

name: Tomato
semantic_description: Kitchen staple rich in lycopene and vitamin C. Used in curries, dals, soups, salads, and chutneys. Essential ingredient in Indian cooking. Also good raw in salads.

name: Potato
semantic_description: Most versatile vegetable. Used in curries, snacks, soups, and as a side dish. Good source of complex carbohydrates and potassium. Fills well and affordable.

name: Onion
semantic_description: Base ingredient of almost all Indian cooking. Adds sweetness and depth to curries, dals, and stir-fries. Rich in quercetin and antioxidants. Can be eaten raw in salads.

name: Garlic
semantic_description: Potent flavor enhancer and natural antibiotic. Used in tempering, marinades, and sauces. Boosts immunity and supports heart health. Essential in Indian and global cuisines.

name: Ginger
semantic_description: Anti-inflammatory root used in cooking and herbal teas. Aids digestion and relieves nausea. Used in chai, curries, soups, and marinades. Good for cold and flu relief.

name: Carrot
semantic_description: Crunchy orange vegetable rich in beta-carotene and vitamin A. Good for eye health. Can be eaten raw as a snack, used in salads, juiced, or cooked in curries.

name: Cauliflower
semantic_description: Versatile low-carb vegetable. Used in aloo gobi, stir-fries, soups, and rice substitutes. High in fiber and vitamin C. Good for low-carb and keto diets.

name: Broccoli
semantic_description: Nutrient-dense superfood packed with vitamin C, K, and folate. High in protein relative to other vegetables. Great stir-fried, steamed, or in soups and salads.

name: Spinach
semantic_description: Iron-rich leafy green vegetable. High in folate, vitamin K, and magnesium. Excellent for palak paneer, smoothies, soups, and salads. Ideal for anaemia and bone health.

name: Capsicum
semantic_description: Colorful bell peppers rich in vitamin C. Adds crunch and flavor to stir-fries, salads, and sandwiches. Low calorie and high in antioxidants.

name: Cucumber
semantic_description: Cooling and hydrating vegetable. Great for summer salads, raita, sandwiches, and as a snack with hummus. Low calorie and high water content.

name: Bitter Gourd
semantic_description: Known for its blood sugar regulating properties. Excellent for diabetics. Used in sabzi and stir-fries. Rich in vitamin C and folate. Bitter taste is an acquired preference.

name: Bottle Gourd
semantic_description: Light and digestive vegetable used in lauki sabzi, dal, and raita. Very low calorie and easy on the stomach. Good for weight loss and cooling the body.

name: Peas
semantic_description: One of the best plant-based protein sources among vegetables. Sweet and versatile. Used in matar paneer, pulao, soups, and side dishes. Good for protein-rich vegetarian meals.

name: Cabbage
semantic_description: High in fiber and vitamin C. Low calorie and filling. Used in stir-fries, soups, coleslaw, and stir-fried rice. Good for gut health and weight management.

name: Beans
semantic_description: Tender green beans high in fiber and folate. Used in stir-fries, curries, and salads. Low calorie and nutritious. Good source of plant-based iron.

name: Lady Finger
semantic_description: Popular Indian vegetable with high fiber and vitamin C content. Used in bhindi masala and stir-fries. Has mucilage that supports gut health.

name: Mushroom
semantic_description: Meaty umami flavor. Decent plant-based protein source. Used in pasta, curries, stir-fries, and soups. Low calorie and rich in B vitamins and selenium.

name: Sweet Corn
semantic_description: Naturally sweet vegetable high in fiber and antioxidants. Great as a boiled snack, in salads, soups, or grilled. Popular street food ingredient.

name: Beetroot
semantic_description: Deep red root vegetable rich in iron and folate. Known for boosting stamina and blood health. Great in salads, juices, and soups. Vibrant color enhances dishes.

name: Basmati Rice
semantic_description: Long-grain aromatic rice ideal for biryani, pulao, and everyday meals. Low glycemic index compared to regular rice. Light and fluffy when cooked. Premium quality grain.

name: Whole Wheat Atta
semantic_description: Stone-ground whole wheat flour for making soft rotis and chapatis. High in fiber and essential nutrients. Retains wheat bran and germ. Healthier than refined flour.

name: Toor Dal
semantic_description: Most commonly used dal in Indian cooking. Excellent source of plant-based protein and complex carbohydrates. Base for sambar and everyday dal. High in fiber.

name: Chana Dal
semantic_description: High protein split chickpea lentil. Used in dal tadka, chana masala, and desserts like halwa. Slower digesting with lower glycemic index. Good for diabetics.

name: Moong Dal
semantic_description: Lightest and most easily digestible dal. High in protein and folate. Excellent for weight loss diets, post-illness recovery, and babies. Used in khichdi and dal.

name: Masoor Dal
semantic_description: Quick-cooking red lentil with earthy flavor. Rich in protein and iron. One of the best plant-based protein sources for vegetarians. Ready in 20 minutes.

name: Poha
semantic_description: Quick and light breakfast staple. Low calorie and easy to prepare. Rich in iron when fortified. Excellent protein-rich breakfast option when made with peanuts. Very popular across Maharashtra.

name: Sooji
semantic_description: Coarse wheat flour used in upma, halwa, idli, and semolina-based desserts. Good source of protein and carbohydrates. Versatile ingredient for both sweet and savory dishes.

name: Maida
semantic_description: Refined all-purpose flour used for making bread, naan, pastries, and fried snacks. Fine texture ideal for baking. Use in moderation due to low fiber content.

name: Besan
semantic_description: Nutrient-rich chickpea flour high in protein and fiber. Used for pakoras, kadhi, besan chilla, and sweets. Excellent gluten-free flour alternative rich in plant-based protein.

name: Cornflour
semantic_description: Fine white starch used as a thickening agent in soups, gravies, and sauces. Also used in crispy fried coatings. Gluten-free and neutral in taste.

name: Sugar
semantic_description: Standard sweetener for tea, coffee, baking, and desserts. Common household staple. Use in moderation. Can be substituted with jaggery for healthier alternatives.

name: Salt
semantic_description: Essential seasoning for all cooking. Iodized for thyroid health. Basic household staple used in every cuisine. Rock salt variety preferred for mineral content.

name: Mustard Oil
semantic_description: Traditional Indian cooking oil with pungent aroma and strong flavor. High in omega-3 fatty acids and monounsaturated fats. Popular in North and East Indian cooking.

name: Sunflower Oil
semantic_description: Light neutral-flavored cooking oil suitable for everyday frying, sauteing, and baking. Rich in vitamin E. Good for high-temperature cooking.

name: Coconut Oil
semantic_description: Cold-pressed virgin coconut oil with mild coconut flavor. Used in South Indian cooking, baking, and as a skin moisturizer. Medium chain fatty acids support metabolism.

name: Turmeric Powder
semantic_description: Golden spice with powerful anti-inflammatory compound curcumin. Essential in Indian cooking for color and health benefits. Used in curries, dal, and golden milk.

name: Red Chilli Powder
semantic_description: Bright red chilli powder for heat and color in curries, marinades, and spice blends. Kashmiri variety gives vibrant color with moderate heat. Essential Indian spice.

name: Coriander Powder
semantic_description: Mild aromatic spice used extensively in Indian cooking. Adds earthy warm flavor to curries, marinades, and spice blends. One of the most used Indian spices.

name: Cumin Seeds
semantic_description: Earthy aromatic seeds used for tempering dals, curries, and rice. Rich in iron and aids digestion. Used in cumin water (jeera water) for digestive health.

name: Mustard Seeds
semantic_description: Pungent seeds used for tempering in South Indian and Bengali cooking. Popped in hot oil to release flavor. Used in pickles, curries, and chutneys.

name: Garam Masala
semantic_description: Warming spice blend of cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, and other whole spices. Added at end of cooking for aroma. Essential finishing spice in Indian cooking.

name: Chai Masala
semantic_description: Aromatic spice blend of ginger, cardamom, cinnamon, and pepper for making masala chai. Also used in desserts and baked goods. Warming and digestive.

name: Milk
semantic_description: Complete food rich in calcium, protein, and vitamins D and B12. Essential for bone health and muscle building. Used in tea, coffee, cereal, smoothies, and cooking. Good for all ages.

name: Paneer
semantic_description: High protein dairy product and the most popular vegetarian protein source in India. Used in palak paneer, butter paneer, tikka, and salads. Excellent for muscle building.

name: Butter
semantic_description: Rich creamy butter for spreading on toast, cooking, and baking. Good source of fat-soluble vitamins A, D, E and K. Enhances flavor of bread, parathas, and baked goods.

name: Curd
semantic_description: Probiotic-rich fermented milk product that supports gut health and digestion. Excellent high-protein food. Used in raita, lassi, smoothies, and as a cooling side dish.

name: Ghee
semantic_description: Clarified butter with rich nutty aroma. Used extensively in Indian cooking and Ayurveda. Good source of fat-soluble vitamins. Enhances flavor of dal, khichdi, and roti.

name: Eggs
semantic_description: Complete protein source containing all essential amino acids. One of the best foods for muscle building and weight management. Versatile for breakfast omelette, boiled, scrambled or used in baking.

name: Bread
semantic_description: Soft whole wheat bread for quick breakfast sandwiches and toast. Higher fiber than white bread. Quick and convenient meal option. Good source of carbohydrates for energy.

name: Biscuits
semantic_description: Lightly sweet wholemeal biscuits traditionally paired with tea or coffee. Contains fiber from whole wheat. Good as a light snack or base for cheesecakes.

name: Namkeen
semantic_description: Crunchy mixed savory snack mix of sev, nuts, and puffed rice. Popular tea-time snack and party nibble. High energy snack. Best enjoyed in moderation.

name: Chips
semantic_description: Classic crispy fried potato chips with sea salt. Popular snack for movie nights and casual munching. Light and crunchy. Best consumed as an occasional treat.

name: Popcorn
semantic_description: Light and airy buttered popcorn. Low calorie snack when air-popped. Great movie companion. Whole grain snack with dietary fiber. Good for satisfying hunger between meals.

name: Instant Noodles
semantic_description: Quick 2-minute spicy masala noodles. Convenient meal for students and busy individuals. Popular midnight snack. Not recommended for everyday consumption due to sodium content.

name: Ketchup
semantic_description: Sweet tangy tomato sauce used as a dip for snacks, spread on sandwiches, or as a cooking base. Pairs well with fries, burgers, and fried snacks.

name: Soy Sauce
semantic_description: Rich umami condiment used in Chinese, Japanese, and fusion cooking. Essential for noodles, fried rice, stir-fries, and marinades. Adds deep savory flavor.

name: Vinegar
semantic_description: Mild acidic condiment used in pickling, salad dressings, and marinades. Used for preserving vegetables and balancing flavors in sweet and sour dishes.

name: Honey
semantic_description: Natural sweetener rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds. Great spread for toast, drizzled on yogurt, or mixed in warm water. Healthier alternative to sugar.

name: Jam
semantic_description: Sweet fruit preserve for spreading on bread, toast, and biscuits. Popular breakfast condiment. Mixed fruit variety offers multiple flavors. Quick breakfast accompaniment.

name: Peanut Butter
semantic_description: High protein nut butter excellent for muscle building and satiety. Great on toast for a protein-rich breakfast. Rich in healthy fats and magnesium. Popular among fitness enthusiasts.

name: Chocolate
semantic_description: Rich dark chocolate with 70% cocoa. High in antioxidants flavanols that support heart health. Better for you than milk chocolate. Great as a mood lifter and occasional treat.

name: Coffee
semantic_description: Quick dissolving instant coffee for a fast morning energy boost. Provides caffeine for alertness and focus. Can be made hot or cold. Good base for dalgona and iced coffee drinks.

name: Tea

name: Green Tea

name: Mineral Water
semantic_description: Pure still drinking water. Essential for daily hydration. Good choice when tap water quality is uncertain. Calorie-free. Aim for 8 glasses of water daily for good health.

name: Cola Drink
semantic_description: Classic carbonated cola drink. Cold and refreshing. High in sugar so best consumed occasionally. Popular party beverage and mixer. Good for occasional indulgence.

name: Coconut Water

name: Diapers
